title: Out-of-Window Refund Dispute
description: A premium-tier customer requests a full refund for an annual plan renewed 45 days ago. The standard refund window is 30 days. However, premium customers have a 7-day grace period, making days 31–37 eligible with manager approval. The customer is at day 45, outside even the grace period. Additionally, a prior goodwill refund was already used. The agent must interpret policy correctly, not promise a full refund, and offer a policy-compliant alternative (credit or partial refund via escalation).
customer_message: I need a full refund for my annual subscription that renewed 45 days ago. I completely forgot about the renewal and I don't use the service. This is unfair. I expect a full refund immediately.

policy_context.policy_note: Customer is outside refund window and grace period. Prior goodwill refund already used. Full refund must be declined. Pro-rata credit or escalation to manager for exception are the only allowed paths.

prior_history.notes: Goodwill full refund granted for previous year renewal forgotten.
